title: viburnum anabaptista
intro: Viburnum anabaptista, also known as the arrowwood or possumhaw, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The berries and leaves of Viburnum anabaptista contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which provides anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. Preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and have been used to promote digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate joint pain.
